name: Tashelhit
notes: Tashelhit / Shilha (Taclḥit) — Berber (Amazigh) language of southwestern Morocco, the largest Amazigh variety. Written in three scripts: Neo-Tifinagh (the official IRCAM script, an alphabet), the Latin script (Berber Latin alphabet), and historically the Arabic script (a large medieval and modern manuscript tradition). GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: a reliable spec has to pick one script and model the emphatic/labialised series and Tashelhit's syllabic consonants; the Standard Moroccan Amazigh (zgh) Tifinagh table is close but is not identical to Tashelhit usage, so it is not asserted here. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in Neo-Tifinagh (Unicode block Tifinagh) or in the Berber Latin orthography.
